title: Leucojum nebrodense
taxonomy: Leucojum nebrodense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Allioideae, which contains plants related to onions and garlic. The genus Leucojum is a group of bulbous perennials, commonly known as snowdrops. Leucojum nebrodense is native to Sicily and is a rare species found in this region. It is often cultivated for its unique and delicate white flowers that bloom in early spring.
